Investors Compensation Scheme Ltd v West Bromwich Building Society Curated authority in the lineage 'Contractual Interpretation: "Context vs. Text"'. Originating UK authority for Lord Hoffmann's contextual principles.

metadata.label: Investors Compensation Scheme Ltd v West Bromwich Building Society

document: Sinoearn International Ltd v Hyundai-CCECC Joint Venture Curated authority in the lineage 'Contractual Interpretation: "Context vs. Text"'. Hong Kong CFA endorsement of the contextual approach.

metadata.label: Sinoearn International Ltd v Hyundai-CCECC Joint Venture

document: Rainy Sky SA v Kookmin Bank Curated authority in the lineage 'Contractual Interpretation: "Context vs. Text"'. Commercial common-sense peak in the UK line.

metadata.label: Rainy Sky SA v Kookmin Bank

document: Fully Profit (Asia) Ltd v Secretary for Justice Curated authority in the lineage 'Contractual Interpretation: "Context vs. Text"'. Hong Kong treatment following Rainy Sky.

metadata.label: Fully Profit (Asia) Ltd v Secretary for Justice

document: Arnold v Britton Curated authority in the lineage 'Contractual Interpretation: "Context vs. Text"'. Textualist correction emphasizing contractual language.
Sany Heavy Industry (HK) Ltd v Sany-Kenton (HK) Ltd Curated authority in the lineage 'Contractual Interpretation: "Context vs. Text"'. Recent Hong Kong application of the textualist correction.

metadata.label: Sany Heavy Industry (HK) Ltd v Sany-Kenton (HK) Ltd

document: Wood v Capita Curated authority in the lineage 'Contractual Interpretation: "Context vs. Text"'. Modern unitary synthesis balancing text and context.
Stilk v Myrick Curated authority in the lineage 'Consideration and Variations: "Practical Benefit"'. Traditional existing-duty rule.
Williams v Roffey Bros & Nicholls Curated authority in the lineage 'Consideration and Variations: "Practical Benefit"'. UK practical-benefit exception to the traditional rule.
UBC (Construction) Ltd v Sung Foo Kee Ltd Curated authority in the lineage 'Consideration and Variations: "Practical Benefit"'. Hong Kong adoption of Williams v Roffey.

metadata.label: UBC (Construction) Ltd v Sung Foo Kee Ltd

document: Re Selectmove Curated authority in the lineage 'Consideration and Variations: "Practical Benefit"'. Refused to extend practical benefit to part-payment of debt.
Chong Cheng Lin Courtney v Cathay Pacific Airways Ltd Curated authority in the lineage 'Consideration and Variations: "Practical Benefit"'. Hong Kong follow-on treatment of the Selectmove limitation.

metadata.label: Chong Cheng Lin Courtney v Cathay Pacific Airways Ltd

document: MWB Business Exchange Centres Ltd v Rock Advertising Ltd Curated authority in the lineage 'Consideration and Variations: "Practical Benefit"'. Illustrates continuing caution in this area.

metadata.label: MWB Business Exchange Centres Ltd v Rock Advertising Ltd

document: Dunlop Pneumatic Tyre Co Ltd v New Garage & Motor Co Ltd Curated authority in the lineage 'Penalty Clauses: "Modern Test"'. Classic genuine pre-estimate versus deterrent framing.

metadata.label: Dunlop Pneumatic Tyre Co Ltd v New Garage & Motor Co Ltd

document: Cavendish Square Holding BV v El Makdessi Curated authority in the lineage 'Penalty Clauses: "Modern Test"'. Modern legitimate-interest and proportionality test.

metadata.label: Cavendish Square Holding BV v El Makdessi

document: Law Ting Pong Secondary School v Chen Wai-mick Curated authority in the lineage 'Penalty Clauses: "Modern Test"'. Hong Kong adoption of the Makdessi test.

metadata.label: Law Ting Pong Secondary School v Chen Wai-mick

document: China Great Wall AMC (International) Holdings Co Ltd v Royal-Investment Group Corp Curated authority in the lineage 'Penalty Clauses: "Modern Test"'. Hong Kong application refining the Law Ting Pong line.

metadata.label: China Great Wall AMC (International) Holdings Co Ltd v Royal-Investment Group Corp

document: Royal Bank of Scotland v Etridge (No 2) Curated authority in the lineage 'Undue Influence: "Etridge"'. Set the modern bank-procedure approach to constructive notice.

metadata.label: Royal Bank of Scotland v Etridge (No 2)

document: Li Sau Keung v Bank of China (Hong Kong) Ltd Curated authority in the lineage 'Undue Influence: "Etridge"'. Hong Kong follow-on application.

metadata.label: Li Sau Keung v Bank of China (Hong Kong) Ltd

document: Unconscionable Contracts Ordinance (Cap. 458) Curated statutory authority in the lineage 'Unconscionability: "Statutory"'. Statutory foundation of the Hong Kong doctrine.
Shum Kong v Chan Man-fai Curated authority in the lineage 'Unconscionability: "Statutory"'. Seminal Hong Kong interpretation of the Ordinance.
Hongkong Fir Shipping Co Ltd v Kawasaki Kisen Kaisha Ltd Curated authority in the lineage 'Breach and Termination: "Intermediate Terms"'. Foundational innominate-term authority with Hong Kong facts.

metadata.label: Hongkong Fir Shipping Co Ltd v Kawasaki Kisen Kaisha Ltd

document: Billion Star Development Ltd v Wong Tak-fai Curated authority in the lineage 'Breach and Termination: "Intermediate Terms"'. Modern Hong Kong application on termination rights.

metadata.label: Billion Star Development Ltd v Wong Tak-fai

document: Donoghue v Stevenson Curated authority in the lineage 'Negligence: Duty of Care'. House of Lords — neighbour principle; duty of care owed to those who ought reasonably to be in contemplation as affected by acts or omissions.

Anns v Merton London Borough Council Curated authority in the lineage 'Negligence: Duty of Care'. Two-stage test: sufficient proximity? Any policy reasons to limit duty? Enabled significant expansion of duty.

metadata.label: Anns v Merton London Borough Council

document: Caparo Industries plc v Dickman Curated authority in the lineage 'Negligence: Duty of Care'. House of Lords — three-stage test: (1) foreseeable harm, (2) proximity, (3) fair just and reasonable. Replaced Anns incrementalism.

Luen Hing Fat Cooked Food Stall v Yau Tsim Mong District Board Curated authority in the lineage 'Negligence: Duty of Care'. Hong Kong Court of Final Appeal applied Caparo three-stage test as the applicable framework.

metadata.label: Luen Hing Fat Cooked Food Stall v Yau Tsim Mong District Board

document: Associated Provincial Picture Houses Ltd v Wednesbury Corporation Curated authority in the lineage 'Judicial Review: From Wednesbury to Proportionality'. Court of Appeal — unreasonableness: decision so unreasonable that no reasonable authority could ever have come to it.

metadata.label: Associated Provincial Picture Houses Ltd v Wednesbury Corporation

document: Council of Civil Service Unions v Minister for Civil Service Curated authority in the lineage 'Judicial Review: From Wednesbury to Proportionality'. House of Lords — three grounds of review: illegality, irrationality, procedural impropriety.

metadata.label: Council of Civil Service Unions v Minister for Civil Service

document: Hysan Development Co Ltd v Town Planning Board Curated authority in the lineage 'Judicial Review: From Wednesbury to Proportionality'. Hong Kong Court of Final Appeal — proportionality test applies for BOR/Basic Law rights; Wednesbury applies for non-rights matters.

metadata.label: Hysan Development Co Ltd v Town Planning Board

document: Leung Kwok Hung v HKSAR Curated authority in the lineage 'Judicial Review: From Wednesbury to Proportionality'. Court of Final Appeal applied proportionality to freedom of assembly restrictions under Basic Law.

O'Neill v Phillips Curated authority in the lineage 'Unfair Prejudice and Minority Shareholder Protection'. House of Lords — unfair prejudice requires conduct unfair in a commercial sense; legitimate expectations from informal understanding enforceable.

Re Kong Thai Sawmill (Miri) Sdn Bhd Curated authority in the lineage 'Unfair Prejudice and Minority Shareholder Protection'. Hong Kong Privy Council — just and equitable winding up; loss of mutual confidence in small quasi-partnership company.

metadata.label: Re Kong Thai Sawmill (Miri) Sdn Bhd

document: Re Kam Kwan Lai Curated authority in the lineage 'Unfair Prejudice and Minority Shareholder Protection'. Hong Kong Court of First Instance — applied Companies Ordinance s.724 unfair prejudice; buyout remedy ordered.

Re Chime Communications Ltd Curated authority in the lineage 'Unfair Prejudice and Minority Shareholder Protection'. Court of Appeal — scope of unfair prejudice petition under Cap.622; management exclusion and valuation principles.

White v White Curated authority in the lineage 'Ancillary Relief: From Discretion to Equality'. House of Lords — yardstick of equality as check on discretion; no bias against non-money earner; outcome should not discriminate.

Miller v Miller; McFarlane v McFarlane Curated authority in the lineage 'Ancillary Relief: From Discretion to Equality'. House of Lords — sharing principle, compensation principle, and needs; sharing based on equal partnership of marriage.

metadata.label: Miller v Miller; McFarlane v McFarlane

document: LKW v DD Curated authority in the lineage 'Ancillary Relief: From Discretion to Equality'. Hong Kong Court of Final Appeal — adopted White/Miller framework; equal sharing as starting point; departure requires good reason.

SPH v SA Curated authority in the lineage 'Ancillary Relief: From Discretion to Equality'. Hong Kong Court of Appeal applied LKW v DD — factors justifying departure from equal sharing in long and short marriages.
